# alertpaca

> Proactive server health checker. Single Rust binary, terminal UI. Checks system resources, services, backups, TLS certificates, and open ports. Reports what's about to go wrong.

## Quick start

Requires Rust toolchain. Install from source:

    git clone https://github.com/fabioconcina/alertpaca
    cd alertpaca && cargo build --release
    ./target/release/alertpaca

## CLI modes

### Interactive TUI (default)

    alertpaca [-c path/to/config.toml]

Full-screen terminal UI with auto-refresh (60s), keyboard navigation, and splash screen.

Key bindings: q quit; r refresh; up/k scroll up; down/j scroll down.

### JSON output (--json)

    alertpaca --json

Single run, JSON array to stdout. Suitable for piping to jq, scripts, or AI agents.

Output format:

    [
      {
        "section": "System",
        "name": "cpu",
        "status": "Ok",
        "summary": "12% usage (4 cores)"
      }
    ]

Status values: Ok, Warning, Critical, Skipped.
Section values: System, Services, Backups, Certificates, Ports.

### Human-readable table (--once)

    alertpaca --once

Single run, plain-text table to stdout.

### MCP server (--mcp)

    alertpaca --mcp

Runs an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server on stdio. Exposes a `check_health` tool.

Tool: check_health
  Parameters: none
  Returns: JSON array of check result objects (same schema as --json output)

Claude Desktop configuration example:

    {
      "mcpServers": {
        "alertpaca": {
          "command": "/path/to/alertpaca",
          "args": ["--mcp"]
        }
      }
    }

## Exit codes

- 0: all checks passed (Ok or Skipped)
- 1: error (config load failure, I/O error)
- 2: at least one check returned Warning or Critical (--json and --once only)

## Flags

    -c, --config <path>  Path to config file (default: ~/.config/alertpaca/config.toml)
    --json               Single run, JSON output, exit
    --once               Single run, text table output, exit
    --mcp                Run as MCP server (stdio)
    --version            Print version and exit
    -h, --help           Show help

## What it checks

- CPU usage: warn >80%, critical >95%
- Memory usage: warn >80%, critical >95%
- Disk usage: warn >80%, critical >90%, with fill-rate prediction
- System load: warn > cores, critical > 2x cores
- Uptime: informational
- Systemd failed units: critical if any failed (Linux only)
- Docker containers: warn if unhealthy/restarting (Linux only)
- Backup freshness: configurable via config.toml (file glob, restic, zfs)
- TLS certificate expiry: warn <30d, critical <7d
- Port/service drift: warn if listeners disappear (Linux only)

## Configuration

Optional. Create ~/.config/alertpaca/config.toml:

    [[backup]]
    name = "documents"
    type = "file"
    path = "/mnt/backup/docs"
    pattern = "backup-*.tar.gz"
    max_age = "24h"

    [[backup]]
    name = "photos"
    type = "restic"
    repo = "/mnt/backup/restic-photos"
    max_age = "7d"

    [[backup]]
    name = "tank/data"
    type = "zfs"
    dataset = "tank/data"
    max_age = "1h"

    [[certificate]]
    endpoint = "nextcloud.home.lan:443"

## State files

Stored in ~/.local/share/alertpaca/:

- history.json: disk usage history for fill-rate prediction
- ports.json: last known listening ports for drift detection
